notes: This batch begins the runner-only residual-class campaign after paired-family completion.
The six declared harden result surfaces are intentionally deferred to the result-only residual class and are not source members here.
possible_downstream_consequence_policy: possible downstream consequence only; no direct A2-1 or A1 promotion from this batch

comparison_anchors.relevance: Classifies these two scripts as runner-only residuals and separately lists their six declared outputs as result-only residuals.
Negative visibility anchor showing no harden-strip runner names or SIM_IDs appear in the top-level sims catalog.
Negative visibility anchor showing no harden-strip runner names or SIM_IDs appear in the repo-held top-level evidence pack.
Provides continuity from the completed residual paired-family campaign into the runner-only residual class.

runtime_expectations: run_axis12_harden_triple_v1.py writes three versioned result files plus one sim_evidence_pack.txt containing three SIM_EVIDENCE blocks.
run_axis12_harden_v2_triple.py writes three versioned result files plus one sim_evidence_pack.txt containing three SIM_EVIDENCE blocks.
The two scripts share the same four-sequence axis12 lattice, the same three-point sweep, and the same theta and cycle count, but v2 raises num_states from 256 to 512.
Sequential execution in one working directory would overwrite sim_evidence_pack.txt across versions.
The six declared result filenames are deferred to the next result-only residual-class pass.
failure_modes: Mistaking the closure-audit runner-only label for absence of declared outputs.
Treating v2 as a simple rename rather than a contract change in storage and negative-control behavior.
Assuming repo-top omission means the harden strip is inactive or trivial.
Merging the six orphan result surfaces into this batch and breaking residual-class separation.
Missing the shared sim_evidence_pack.txt overwrite hazard.

sims_split_rule.notes: This batch remains inside the separate sims source class and does not mix with ordinary theory/doc extraction.
